title: SpanSaver · Telemetry Cost
description: Infra-side observability waste for the victim stack — the exact signals the T1/T3/T4 detectors act on, so the dashboard and the auditor tell the same story. Log bytes by service (T1 debug flood), DEBUG vs INFO over time, health-check span share (T3), and metric cardinality per name (T4). Queries use CONFIRMED columns only (auditor/telemetry_auditor/schema.py, VERIFIED 2026-07-21): logs_v2 timestamp is UInt64 nanoseconds; signoz_index_v3 timestamp is DateTime64; time_series_v4 unix_milli is epoch millis. If this build rejects the import, rebuild panels from dashboards/README.md (same queries).

widgets.title: Total log bytes (window)
widgets.description: sum(length(body)) across all services over the selected range — the ingest the T1 filter trims.

clickhouse_sql.name: A
clickhouse_sql.query: SELECT sum(length(body)) AS value FROM signoz_logs.distributed_logs_v2 WHERE timestamp >= {{.start_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000 AND timestamp <= {{.end_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000

widgets.title: Log volume by service (T1)
widgets.description: Bytes, DEBUG lines, and total lines per service. A high DEBUG share on a hot service is the debug-flood leak.

clickhouse_sql.name: A
clickhouse_sql.query: SELECT resources_string['service.name'] AS service, sum(length(body)) AS log_bytes, countIf(severity_number > 0 AND severity_number < 9) AS debug_lines, count() AS total_lines FROM signoz_logs.distributed_logs_v2 WHERE timestamp >= {{.start_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000 AND timestamp <= {{.end_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000 GROUP BY service ORDER BY log_bytes DESC

widgets.title: DEBUG vs INFO+ log lines over time
widgets.description: OTel severity 1-8 (DEBUG/TRACE) vs 9+ (INFO and above). After the T1 apply the DEBUG series steps to ~0 while INFO+ keeps flowing.

clickhouse_sql.name: A
clickhouse_sql.query: SELECT toStartOfInterval(toDateTime(timestamp / 1000000000), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE) AS ts, countIf(severity_number > 0 AND severity_number < 9) AS value FROM signoz_logs.distributed_logs_v2 WHERE timestamp >= {{.start_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000 AND timestamp <= {{.end_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000 GROUP BY ts ORDER BY ts

clickhouse_sql.name: B
clickhouse_sql.query: SELECT toStartOfInterval(toDateTime(timestamp / 1000000000), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE) AS ts, countIf(severity_number >= 9) AS value FROM signoz_logs.distributed_logs_v2 WHERE timestamp >= {{.start_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000 AND timestamp <= {{.end_timestamp_ms}} * 1000000 GROUP BY ts ORDER BY ts

widgets.title: Spans by route — health-check spam (T3)
widgets.description: Span count and error count per httpRoute. Probe routes (/healthz) dominating with 0 errors are the waste; error-status probes are KEPT as signal.

clickhouse_sql.name: A
clickhouse_sql.query: SELECT httpRoute AS route, count() AS spans, countIf(has_error) AS errors FROM signoz_traces.distributed_signoz_index_v3 WHERE httpRoute != '' AND timestamp >= fromUnixTimestamp64Milli({{.start_timestamp_ms}}) AND timestamp <= fromUnixTimestamp64Milli({{.end_timestamp_ms}}) GROUP BY route ORDER BY spans DESC LIMIT 20

widgets.title: Active series per metric — cardinality (T4)
widgets.description: uniqExact(fingerprint) per metric_name = active series. A single metric exploded to thousands of series (a per-user label) is the cardinality bomb; the fix drops the label, keeping the metric.

clickhouse_sql.name: A
clickhouse_sql.query: SELECT metric_name, uniqExact(fingerprint) AS active_series FROM signoz_metrics.distributed_time_series_v4 WHERE unix_milli >= {{.start_timestamp_ms}} AND unix_milli <= {{.end_timestamp_ms}} GROUP BY metric_name ORDER BY active_series DESC LIMIT 20
